purpose: Define the shared multi-site storefront experience foundation so AI can evolve site-specific frontend design without rewriting core commerce UX.
principles: Use one shared storefront code foundation for routing, cart, checkout, order lookup, account access, analytics, privacy consent, responsive behavior, and accessibility basics.
Keep site personality in profile-level theme, content, and experience configuration.
Use semantic sections instead of arbitrary visual slicing. AI may change section order, variants, and copy through profiles before adding one-off page code.
Target stable 80-point product design: clear, trustworthy, modern, and usable before award-level visual novelty.
Preserve purchase-to-delivery and order-recovery paths when changing visual design.
Every current and future storefront page should have an experience contract before it receives site-specific layout behavior.
sharedFoundationOwns: cart, checkout, payment attempt, and order access UX
sitePersonalizationOwns: brand name, locale, currency, timezone, and domains
theme colors, density, radius, and future typography tokens
page intent, layout strategy, section order, and section variants
content seed entries and editorial tone

sectionTypes.purpose: State the site promise and expose primary product and order lookup actions.
Expose configured or derived product groups.
Show purchasable products using the shared product card system.
Use public content to support trust, education, and discovery when content exists.
State catalog scope and buyer expectations.
Support category filtering and sort.
Render browsable products.
Show product imagery or fallback identity.
Show price, availability, delivery facts, and add-to-cart action.
Explain product and fulfillment details.
Show cart lines.
Summarize totals and expose checkout action.
Collect checkout inputs with clear errors.
Keep order totals and payment state visible.
Recover guest order access.
List published content entries.
Render one readable content entry.
Support optional account login and registration.
Show optional account profile and order links.
Recover optional account access.
Explain delivery, recovery, or support expectations.

preferredFirstMoves: change profile experience page intent, layout, section order, or section variant names
adjust profile theme and content copy
connect an existing page to the shared section renderer contract
add a new semantic section type only when existing sections cannot express the page goal
doNotDoWithoutExplicitReason: rewrite checkout or order lookup for visual reasons
hard-code site-specific copy in shared components
add decorative sections that do not support discovery, trust, checkout, support, or content strategy
copy an external site's protected visual design
